headlines.title: Jeremy Bowen: Ceasefire means respite for civilians, but it might not last long
headlines.description: Whether or not a lasting peace deal can be reached, the war and its consequences are reshaping the Middle East, writes international editor Jeremy Bowen.

headlines.title: Trump criticises Nato as alliance chief describes meeting as 'very frank'
headlines.description: The US president says Nato "wasn't there when we needed them" during the Iran war.

headlines.title: BBC at the site of Israeli air strikes in Beirut
headlines.description: At least 182 people were killed and 890 others wounded, according to the Lebanese health ministry.

headlines.title: Israel says it hit Lebanon 100 times in just 10 minutes
headlines.description: Israel described it as the largest wave of air strikes in this conflict, hitting more than 100 of what it called Hezbollah command centres and military sites.

headlines.title: Farmers warn of higher food costs due to Iran war
headlines.description: Despite a ceasefire, the Iran war will continue to affect farmers' costs and therefore food prices.

headlines.title: 22,000 students told to pay back 'mis-sold' maintenance loans
headlines.description: Those affected have been told their courses were never eligible for maintenance or childcare loans

headlines.title: Cooper urges full and toll-free reopening of Strait of Hormuz
headlines.description: The foreign secretary also calls for Lebanon to be "urgently included" in the ceasefire agreement.

headlines.title: Four people die in Channel crossing attempt, French authorities say
headlines.description: Rescue efforts remain under way after the incident off the coast of northern France, local media is reporting.

headlines.title: Artemis crew returning to Earth with 'all the good stuff' from Moon discoveries
headlines.description: The four astronauts said they were returning to Earth with "so many more pictures, so many more stories".

headlines.title: Oil prices rise as concerns grow over 'fragile' US-Iran ceasefire
headlines.description: Crude prices have reversed from sharp falls on Wednesday when plans to pause the war first emerged.

headlines.title: Pioneering wildlife cameraman Doug Allan dies in Nepal
headlines.description: The cameraman and photographer won eight Emmy Awards for his work on acclaimed series like Blue Planet alongside Sir David Attenborough.

headlines.title: RSPCA says 250 dogs found crammed into one home
headlines.description: The animal charity says it has been forced to refute that an image of the dogs was AI-generated.

headlines.title: Primrose Hill stab victim 'can never be replaced', father says
headlines.description: Christopher Sullivan pays tribute to his son, filmmaking student Finbar Sullivan, who was killed.

headlines.title: New light shed on who benefits most from weight-loss jabs
headlines.description: People who carry variations in two genes linked to appetite and digestion can lose more weight when taking drugs to treat obesity, research suggests.

headlines.title: Chinese researcher's death after questioning in US prompts anger in Beijing
headlines.description: The semiconductor researcher was found dead after “hostile questioning” by US law enforcement.

headlines.title: 'Ketamine Queen' sentenced to 15 years over Matthew Perry overdose death
headlines.description: Jasveen Sangha was found guilty of selling drugs that killed Friends actor Matthew Perry who had struggled with addiction for years.

headlines.title: Liverpool still alive - but their season now hangs by a thread
headlines.description: Negative tactics and no shots on target - but somehow Liverpool's Champions League campaign is still just about alive after a 2-0 loss at PSG.

headlines.title: Five of Queen Elizabeth II's most iconic outfits - from new royal exhibit
headlines.description: From fashion diplomacy in the US to a transparent raincoat, the late Queen's style was a powerful formula.

headlines.title: He's Australia's most decorated soldier. Now he's at the centre of a historic war crimes case
headlines.description: Ben Roberts-Smith's case is not only unprecedented for Australia but "extraordinary" for the globe too, scholars say.

headlines.title: Is BTS losing its K-pop identity as it aims for a bigger world stage?
headlines.description: BTS turned millions on to K-pop. But now it’s caught between Korea and the world.

headlines.title: The Papers: 'Fragile ceasefire at risk' and 'Putin mocks Starmer'
headlines.description: The papers on Thursday focus on the aftermath of the two-week ceasefire deal agreed between the US, Israel and Iran.

headlines.title: Euphoria stars hit red carpet at premiere of third - and possibly final - season
headlines.description: Sydney Sweeney, Zendaya and Jacob Elordi reunite and are joined by some new cast members at the launch.

headlines.title: 'Cold as ice': Serial killer admits to eight murders in case that haunted Long Island for years
headlines.description: Rex Heuermann's plea ends a case that police took 13 years to solve, frustrating the public and victims' family.

headlines.title: Watch: Pope Leo XIV spins basketball on his finger, with help from Harlem Globetrotters
headlines.description: US exhibition squad, the Harlem Globetrotters, met the pontiff in the Vatican, helping him to spin a basketball on the tip of his finger.

headlines.title: US man arrested in case of Michigan woman's disappearance in Bahamas
headlines.description: Lynette Hooker fell overboard from a small boat and was swept out to sea by strong currents, her husband told authorities.

headlines.title: Jim Whittaker, first American to Mount Everest summit, dies aged 97
headlines.description: The lifelong outdoors advocate and former president of retailer REI returned to Everest decades later with his son.

headlines.title: 'Titan of a man': Tributes to actor with MND who played Richard III in wheelchair
headlines.description: Michael Campbell, also known as Michael Patrick, died on Tuesday at NI Hospice.

headlines.title: BBC upholds complaints over racial slur in Baftas broadcast
headlines.description: The broadcast of a racial slur broke editorial standards, the corporation's complaints unit concludes.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: US-Iran Ceasefire Agreed (For Now)
headlines.description: US & Iran agree a ceasefire but how long will it last?

headlines.title: Trump pulls back from the brink...but for how long?
headlines.description: Trump’s threat to “wipe out” Iran has divided Republicans.

headlines.title: How fluid PSG exposed Slot's back-five tweak
headlines.description: Arne Slot changed Liverpool's shape for their Champions League quarter-final first leg - but their tactics were still bested by European champions Paris St-Germain. Umir Irfan explains how.

headlines.title: Verstappen race engineer Lambiase to join McLaren
headlines.description: Red Bull's Gianpiero Lambiase, Max Verstappen's race engineer, agrees to join McLaren from 2028.

headlines.title: Fury unlikely to be world champion again - Wardley
headlines.description: Tyson Fury will struggle to become world champion again because of the current crop of belt holders, says WBO title holder Fabio Wardley.

headlines.title: Family fun and holes-in-one - the Masters tradition crossing generations
headlines.description: The Masters Par 3 contest is a unique tradition that features 90-year-old Gary Player holing birdie putts and nine-year-old Frankie Fleetwood focusing on hitting a green.

headlines.title: Alvarez scores superb free-kick as Atletico beat 10-man Barca
headlines.description: Julian Alvarez and Alexander Sorloth give Atletico Madrid a 2-0 victory over 10-man Barcelona in their Champions League quarter-final first leg at the Nou Camp.

headlines.title: 'I don't know how this happened' - Bell on leading England's attack
headlines.description: Lauren Bell discusses the speed of her rise to Women's Premier League winner and the leader of England's pace attack.
